title: 11 Herbal Creams For Gastroparesis
intro_desc: If you're living with gastroparesis, a condition where your stomach takes too long to empty its contents, herbal creams can be a game-changer. These creams typically contain natural ingredients like Curcuma longa, Zingiber officinale, and Glycyrrhiza glabra, which are known for their anti-inflammatory properties.  For example, Curcuma longa, commonly known as turmeric, contains a compound called curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe your stomach and reduce inflammation. Similarly, Zingiber officinale, or ginger, has natural anti-inflammatory compounds that can help ease nausea and bloating, common symptoms of gastroparesis. Glycyrrhiza glabra, or licorice root, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect your stomach lining from damage.  Herbal creams for gastroparesis work by providing a topical application of these natural ingredients, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the stomach. By applying these creams to the skin, the active ingredients can be absorbed into the body and reach the stomach, where they can provide relief from symptoms. This can be especially helpful for people who have difficulty taking oral medications or experience side effects from traditional treatments.  Applying herbal creams can also be a calming and soothing experience, which can be especially beneficial for people with gastroparesis who often experience anxiety and stress related to their condition. By incorporating herbal creams into your daily routine, you may find that your symptoms improve and you're able to manage your gastroparesis more effectively.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains bioactive compounds like curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin. These compounds have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Curcumin, in particular, has been shown to stimulate digestive enzymes and improve gut motility, which can help alleviate gastroparesis symptoms. The anti-inflammatory properties of these compounds can also help reduce inflammation in the stomach and small intestine, further contributing to improved digestive function. By reducing inflammation and improving gut motility, Curcuma longa creams may provide relief from gastroparesis symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for gastroparesis
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 teaspoons of Curcuma longa powder in a double boiler.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in the double boiler over low heat, stirring occasionally.
Add the vitamin E oil and Curcuma longa powder to the melted mixture. Stir well to combine.
Continue heating the mixture for another 10-15 minutes, or until it reaches 120-130°F (49-54°C).
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool until it thickens and turns into a creamy texture. Pour into containers and store in the fridge.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains active constituents like gingerol and shogaol. These compounds help to stimulate digestion and improve gut motility, which is often impaired in gastroparesis. Gingerols and shogaols also have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and promote healing. Additionally, the bioactive compounds in Zingiber officinale creams have been shown to relax the smooth muscle in the stomach, allowing food to move more easily through the digestive system. This relaxation of the stomach muscles can help to alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis, such as nausea and vomiting.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for gastroparesis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 10 grams of Zingiber officinale (ginger) powder.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add vitamin E oil and stir well. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Add ginger powder to the mixture and stir until well combined. Pour into a glass jar or tin.
Let the mixture cool and solidify completely before using. Store in a cool, dry place and use as needed to help alleviate gastroparesis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains active constituents like glycyrrhetic acid and glycosides. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is often a problem for people with gastroparesis. Glycyrrhetic acid also has antioxidant properties that help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, glycyrrhizin, a compound in Glycyrrhiza glabra, has been shown to stimulate digestion and improve gut motility, which can help alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis. By reducing inflammation and promoting digestion, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may provide relief for people experiencing gastroparesis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for gastroparesis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1/4 cup of glycerin, 2 tablespoons of glycyrrhiza glabra extract, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil.
Combine 1 cup of distilled water and 1/4 cup of glycerin in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture on low heat until the glycerin dissolves.
Add 2 tablespoons of glycyrrhiza glabra extract to the mixture and stir well. Remove the saucepan from the heat.
In a separate bowl, mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter until well combined. Add the mixture to the glycyrrhiza glabra extract mixture and stir until smooth.
Add 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil and stir well. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, your glycyrrhiza glabra cream is ready for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum creams contains the active constituents cinnamaldehyde, cinnamyl alcohol, and linalool. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract associated with gastroparesis. Cinnamaldehyde also has antioxidant properties, which can help protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress. Additionally, cinnamaldehyde has been shown to improve gastric motility, helping the stomach muscles contract and move food through the digestive system. By reducing inflammation and improving gastric motility, Cinnamomum verum creams may help alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum verum herbal creams for gastroparesis
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the beeswax and stir until it's fully melted. Remove from heat.
Let the mixture cool and thicken for about 30 minutes. Then, stir in the vitamin E oil.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool completely. Store in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are active constituents that help with gastroparesis. Flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, have antioxidant properties that reduce inflammation in the stomach and improve blood flow. Terpenoids, such as bilobalide, have neuroprotective properties that help regulate stomach contractions and motility. These properties help to alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis, including nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. By improving stomach function and reducing inflammation, Ginkgo biloba creams can help manage gastroparesis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for gastroparesis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba extract, 10 drops of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add shea butter and stir until melted. Remove from heat.
Add Ginkgo biloba extract and vitamin E oil. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once set, use as a cream for gastroparesis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare creams contains a compound called anethole, which has been shown to have a relaxing effect on the muscles in the stomach. This relaxation can help improve digestion and reduce symptoms of gastroparesis, a condition where the stomach takes too long to empty its contents. Additionally, Foeniculum vulgare creams contain limonene and beta-pinene, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the stomach lining. The antispasmodic properties of anethole can also help reduce nausea and vomiting associated with gastroparesis. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation of the stomach muscles, Foeniculum vulgare creams may help alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal creams for gastroparesis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Foeniculum vulgare seeds and 2 cups of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil) in a clean glass jar.
Steep the Foeniculum vulgare seeds in the carrier oil for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the seeds.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil to the strained oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments until the beeswax is melted.
Pour the mixture into small glass jars or containers and let it cool and solidify before use as a topical cream for gastroparesis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains carnosic acid, rosmarinic acid, and borneol as its active constituents. These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe the digestive tract and improve digestion in individuals with gastroparesis. Rosmarinic acid, in particular, has been shown to relax the muscles in the stomach, allowing food to move more easily through the digestive system. Carnosic acid has also been found to have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing inflammation in the stomach and promoting the healing of gastric ulcers often associated with gastroparesis. By reducing inflammation and relaxing stomach muscles, Rosmarinus officinalis creams may help alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis, such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for gastroparesis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 teaspoons of vitamin E oil, and 10 drops of Rosmarinus officinalis essential oil.
Combine 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler. Heat until the beeswax melts.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly. Stir in 2 teaspoons of vitamin E oil and 10 drops of Rosmarinus officinalis essential oil.
Slowly add 1 cup of distilled water to the mixture while whisking until smooth. Continue whisking until the mixture cools and thickens.
Pour the cream into a container and store in the fridge for up to 2 weeks. Apply a small amount to the affected area 2-3 times a day as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains several bioactive constituents that can provide relief for gastroparesis. One of the key compounds is aloin, a anthraquinone that helps to stimulate bowel movements by increasing the contractility of the intestinal muscles. Another important compound is aloe-emodin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. Aloe barbadensis creams also contains glycoproteins and polysaccharides that can help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing irritation and inflammation. By stimulating bowel movements and reducing inflammation, aloe barbadensis creams may help to alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis, such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for gastroparesis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup Aloe barbadensis gel, 1/4 cup coconut oil, 2 tablespoons beeswax, 2 tablespoons vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons glycerin.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval until melted.
Add Aloe barbadensis gel, vitamin E oil, and glycerin to the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into small containers or a silicone mold. Let it cool and harden at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Once hardened, label and store the creams in the fridge for up to 2 weeks. Apply as needed to help soothe gastroparesis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams contains the bioactive constituents menthone, limonene, and beta-pinene. These constituents have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the stomach and intestines. The menthol in Mentha x piperita creams has a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help slow down rapid digestion and improve nutrient absorption. Additionally, the antispasmodic properties of menthol can help relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms and cramps that are common with gastroparesis. By reducing inflammation, oxidative stress, and spasms, Mentha x piperita creams can help alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for gastroparesis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups coconut oil, 1 cup shea butter, 1/2 cup beeswax, 2 tablespoons peppermint essential oil, 2 tablespoons vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat, stirring until smooth.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly. Stir in peppermint essential oil and vitamin E oil.
Pour mixture into a container and let cool completely. It will solidify as it cools.
Once solid, use a hand mixer or whisk to whip the cream until light and fluffy. Store in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum creams contains silymarin as its active constituent. Silymarin is a flavonoid compound that has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to protect the stomach lining from damage and inflammation, which is often a concern in gastroparesis. Silymarin also has hepatoprotective properties, which may aid in improving the gut's ability to absorb nutrients and move food through the digestive system. By reducing inflammation and protecting the stomach lining, silymarin in Silybum marianum creams may help alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal creams for gastroparesis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried Silybum marianum flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Combine dried Silybum marianum flowers and coconut oil in a saucepan. Heat over low heat for 2 hours, stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl. Discard the solids.
In a separate saucepan, melt the beeswax over low heat. Add the strained mixture and vitamin E oil. Stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a silicone ice cube tray or a small tin container. Let it cool and harden before using as a cream for gastroparesis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams contains alkaloids, including quinine and quinidine, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, a common issue in gastroparesis. The alkaloids in Cinchona officinalis also have antispasmodic effects, which can help relax the stomach muscles and improve digestion. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis contains kaempferol, a flavonoid that has antioxidant properties, which can protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and protecting the stomach lining, Cinchona officinalis creams may help alleviate some of the symptoms of gastroparesis.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal creams for gastroparesis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of dried Cinchona officinalis bark, and a clean glass jar with a lid.
Combine the water and Cinchona officinalis bark in the glass jar. Stir well to mix.
Steep the mixture for 2 hours in a cool, dark place. Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Combine 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a small saucepan. Heat over low heat, stirring until melted.
Mix the cooled liquid from step 3 with the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture. Pour into a clean container and let cool to create the Cinchona officinalis cream.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent gastroparesis. Some teas, like peppermint and ginger, can ease digestion and reduce nausea. They can also help relax the muscles in the stomach, which can become weak in gastroparesis. Regularly drinking these teas may help keep the stomach functioning normally.
faq_safe: While herbal teas may help ease gastroparesis symptoms, consuming them daily is not always safe. Some herbs can interact with medications or worsen conditions, and quality can vary between brands. It's essential to check the ingredients and potential interactions before making herbal teas a regular part of your gastroparesis treatment plan.
faq_timeline: For people with gastroparesis, herbal teas can help with digestion. Some teas, like peppermint and ginger, can ease nausea and stomach discomfort. Results may start to show within a few hours after drinking the tea. You might notice that you can eat more without feeling sick, or that your body is moving food through your system more easily.
faq_time: For gastroparesis, it's a good idea to drink herbal tea in the morning or early afternoon. This can help stimulate digestion and ease nausea. Drinking tea too close to bedtime might make it harder to fall asleep. Experimenting with different times can help you find what works best for your body.
faq_interaction: Some herbal teas can affect how gastroparesis medications work in your body. For example, peppermint tea can slow down digestion, which might interact with certain medications. Ginger tea can also thin the blood, which might be a problem if you're taking medications that prevent bleeding. It's a good idea to ask about any interactions with your doctor.
faq_pregnant: If you have gastroparesis and are pregnant, it's best to talk to your healthcare provider before drinking herbal teas. Some herbs can affect the baby or worsen your condition. If you do choose to drink herbal teas, look for ones that are specifically labeled as safe for pregnancy. Always check the ingredients and follow the recommended amounts.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding and having gastroparesis, it's essential to be cautious about what you drink. Some herbal teas may help soothe your stomach, but others could interact with medications or worsen symptoms. Look for teas like peppermint, ginger, and fennel, which are generally considered safe and may ease nausea. However, always check the ingredients and potential side effects.
title_2: 11 Herbal Creams for Chemo Patients with Gastroparesis
